comments.body:
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Added SEO capabilities to an Astro project deployed on Cloudflare Pages through sitemap integration, dynamic robots.txt generation via an Astro endpoint, and JSON-LD schema markup. Configuration uses environment variables to generate environment-specific absolute URLs at build time. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Build Configuration**
`astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`|Added `@astrojs/sitemap` dependency and integration with `site` configuration derived from `CF_PAGES_URL` environment variable for dynamic URL generation.| |**SEO Metadata**
`src/layouts/Layout.astro`, `src/pages/robots.txt.ts`|Enhanced Layout component with JSON-LD Restaurant schema and improved OGP/Twitter metadata. Added dynamic `robots.txt` endpoint that constructs sitemap URL from configured site origin.| |**Documentation**
`docs/references/astro-cloudflare-pages-seo/guidelines.md`, `docs/research/2026-04-19-astro-cloudflare-seo-best-practices.md`|Added deployment guidelines and best practices documentation for dynamic SEO configuration with Cloudflare Pages, including environment variable strategies and edge cases.| |**Content Updates**
`src/pages/index.astro`|Updated page title to Japanese localization (`ホーム`).| ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Build as Astro Build System participant Env as Environment Variables participant Config as astro.config.mjs participant Sitemap as Sitemap Integration participant RobotsEndpoint as robots.txt Endpoint participant Layout as Layout Component Build->>Env: Read CF_PAGES_URL Env-->>Config: Return site origin Config->>Sitemap: Configure with site origin Sitemap->>Sitemap: Generate sitemap-index.xml Build->>RobotsEndpoint: Invoke at build time RobotsEndpoint->>Config: Access site configuration Config-->>RobotsEndpoint: Return site origin RobotsEndpoint->>RobotsEndpoint: Build Sitemap URL with site RobotsEndpoint-->>Build: Return robots.txt content Build->>Layout: Render with site context Layout->>Layout: Generate JSON-LD with site URLs Layout-->>Build: Return rendered HTML ``` ## Estimated Code Review Effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~20 minutes ## Poem > 🐰✨ *Hops with glee through meta tags,* > *JSON-LD schemas dance and sag,* > *Sitemap blooms from Pages so blue,* > *robots.txt whispers, "I guide all through,"* > *SEO dreams in the rabbit's delight!* 🌍
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 3
✅ Passed checks (3 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :----------------: | :------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title clearly and specifically describes the main changes: SEO optimization and dynamic URL handling for トラットリアポルト, which aligns with the PR's core objectives of implementing sitemap generation, optimizing metadata, adding JSON-LD schemas, and configuring environment-based URLs. | | Docstring Coverage | ✅ Passed | No functions found in the changed files to evaluate docstring coverage. Skipping docstring coverage check. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feature/seo-optimization`
--- Thanks for using [CodeRabbit](https://coderabbit.ai?utm_source=oss&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=zakizaki-ri9/gridsome-trattoria-site&utm_content=246)! It's free for OSS, and your support helps us grow. If you like it, consider giving us a shout-out.
❤️ Share - [X](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=I%20just%20used%20%40coderabbitai%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20the%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A&url=https%3A//coderabbit.ai) - [Mastodon](https://mastodon.social/share?text=I%20just%20used%20%40coderabbitai%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20the%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fcoderabbit.ai) - [Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/submit?title=Great%20tool%20for%20code%20review%20-%20CodeRabbit&text=I%20just%20used%20CodeRabbit%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20proprietary%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%3A%20https%3A//coderabbit.ai) - [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcoderabbit.ai&mini=true&title=Great%20tool%20for%20code%20review%20-%20CodeRabbit&summary=I%20just%20used%20CodeRabbit%20for%20my%20code%20review%2C%20and%20it%27s%20fantastic%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20OSS%20and%20offers%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20proprietary%20code)

latestReviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 4**
🧹 Nitpick comments (3)
src/pages/robots.txt.ts (1)
`3-8`: **Guard against `site` being undefined.** Astro's `APIRoute` context types `site` as `URL | undefined`. While `astro.config.mjs` currently sets `site`, the handler will throw a `TypeError` if that config is ever cleared or the env fallback resolves to an empty string (e.g., if someone defines `CF_PAGES_URL=''` in a CI env). A defensive check keeps the endpoint robust:
♻️ Suggested change ```diff -export const GET: APIRoute = ({ site }) => { - const robotsTxt = ` +export const GET: APIRoute = ({ site }) => { + if (!site) { + return new Response('Sitemap site URL is not configured', { status: 500 }); + } + const robotsTxt = ` User-agent: * Allow: / Sitemap: ${new URL('sitemap-index.xml', site).href} `.trim(); ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/pages/robots.txt.ts` around lines 3 - 8, The GET APIRoute currently assumes the context site is defined and will throw if site is undefined; update the GET handler (export const GET) to defensively handle a missing or empty site by checking the site parameter (and/or treating it as falsy) before constructing new URL('sitemap-index.xml', site). If site is missing, return a safe default response (e.g., build robotsTxt with a fallback absolute URL or omit Sitemap) or return a 500/400 with a clear message; ensure you reference the site value only after the guard so new URL is not called with undefined. ```
src/pages/index.astro (1)
`8-8`: **Consider a more SEO-friendly homepage title.** With `Layout`'s template, the rendered `` becomes `ホーム | トラットリア ポルト (TRATTORIA PORTO)`. For a homepage, `ホーム` adds no keyword value and pushes the brand to the right — the first ~60 characters matter most in SERPs. A common pattern is a descriptive tagline or location keywords, e.g., `山梨県笛吹市の北イタリア料理店` or letting the layout emit brand-only for the root path. <details> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary> ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/pages/index.astro` at line 8, The homepage title prop passed into the Layout component ("ホーム") is not SEO-friendly; update the Layout usage in index.astro by replacing title="ホーム" with a descriptive, keyword-rich string (e.g., a location + cuisine tagline like "山梨県笛吹市の北イタリア料理店") or remove the title prop so Layout emits only the brand for the root path; modify the Layout invocation (the Layout component and its title prop) accordingly and, if you prefer a programmatic fix, add root-path detection inside the Layout component to emit a brand-only title when no explicit title prop is provided. ``` </details> </blockquote></details> <details> <summary>src/layouts/Layout.astro (1)</summary><blockquote> `20-22`: **Fallback URL duplicates the config default.** The hard-coded fallback `https://trattoria-e-bar-porto-yamanashi.pages.dev` is duplicated from `astro.config.mjs` (Line 8). If the config host ever changes (e.g., custom domain), this copy will silently drift. Since `site` is now always set in config, `Astro.site` should never be undefined at build time; the fallback can be removed, or at least reduced to a dev-only sentinel. <details> <summary>♻️ Suggested change</summary> ```diff -// サイトのURLが未設定の場合のフォールバック -const siteURL = Astro.site || new URL('https://trattoria-e-bar-porto-yamanashi.pages.dev'); +const siteURL = Astro.site!; ``` </details> <details> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary> ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/layouts/Layout.astro` around lines 20 - 22, Remove the hard-coded production fallback URL and rely on Astro.site (which is always set in config); update the code around siteURL/canonicalURL/ogImageURL so siteURL is created directly from Astro.site (or, if you want a dev-only sentinel, use a short clearly-marked DEV_SITE constant guarded by import.meta.env.DEV) instead of new URL('https://trattoria-e-bar-porto-yamanashi.pages.dev'); ensure canonicalURL and ogImageURL continue to be created from that siteURL (referencing siteURL, canonicalURL, ogImageURL and Astro.site) so the value never drifts from astro.config.mjs. ``` </details> </blockquote></details> </blockquote></details> <details> <summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary> ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Inline comments: In `@astro.config.mjs`: - Line 8: The site config currently uses process.env.CF_PAGES_URL as the primary value which yields per-deploy hostnames; change the assignment for the site property so it prefers your stable production hostname (e.g. the canonical *.pages.dev or your CUSTOM_DOMAIN env) and only falls back to process.env.CF_PAGES_URL for previews; update the site property logic (the site symbol in astro.config.mjs that currently references process.env.CF_PAGES_URL) to check for a stable domain first, then use CF_PAGES_URL as the fallback. In `@docs/references/astro-cloudflare-pages-seo/guidelines.md`: - Around line 8-16: The docs example for astro.config.mjs diverges from the actual implementation: update the project's astro.config.mjs so the site value uses the same precedence as the guideline (site: process.env.CUSTOM_DOMAIN || process.env.CF_PAGES_URL || 'http://localhost:4321') or alternatively change the docs sample to match the current implementation (site: process.env.CF_PAGES_URL || 'https://trattoria-e-bar-porto-yamanashi.pages.dev'); modify the file that defines site in astro.config.mjs and ensure the code references process.env.CUSTOM_DOMAIN and process.env.CF_PAGES_URL consistently between doc and code so contributors see the same canonical setup. In `@docs/research/2026-04-19-astro-cloudflare-seo-best-practices.md`: - Around line 17-18: Update the paragraph about Cloudflare Pages and CF_PAGES_URL to clarify that CF_PAGES_URL is set to the unique per-deployment URL (e.g., https://<commit-hash>.<project>.pages.dev) for every build — including production — instead of implying production uses a stable canonical URL; mention the impact on using site: process.env.CF_PAGES_URL in astro.config.mjs and point readers to the existing mitigation suggestion in the doc (see the astro.config.mjs comment) for how to handle canonical/production URLs. In `@src/layouts/Layout.astro`: - Around line 74-92: The JSON-LD block in Layout.astro currently emits a Restaurant schema on every page with an invalid empty "@id"; remove the empty "@id" (either omit the field or set it to the page canonical URL via siteURL) and stop rendering this Restaurant JSON-LD globally — move it to the canonical/homepage template so the LocalBusiness entity is asserted only once; also update "telephone" to E.164 format (e.g., +81-55-234-5256) and ensure "image" uses ogImageURL and "url" uses siteURL when/where you include the block. --- Nitpick comments: In `@src/layouts/Layout.astro`: - Around line 20-22: Remove the hard-coded production fallback URL and rely on Astro.site (which is always set in config); update the code around siteURL/canonicalURL/ogImageURL so siteURL is created directly from Astro.site (or, if you want a dev-only sentinel, use a short clearly-marked DEV_SITE constant guarded by import.meta.env.DEV) instead of new URL('https://trattoria-e-bar-porto-yamanashi.pages.dev'); ensure canonicalURL and ogImageURL continue to be created from that siteURL (referencing siteURL, canonicalURL, ogImageURL and Astro.site) so the value never drifts from astro.config.mjs. In `@src/pages/index.astro`: - Line 8: The homepage title prop passed into the Layout component ("ホーム") is not SEO-friendly; update the Layout usage in index.astro by replacing title="ホーム" with a descriptive, keyword-rich string (e.g., a location + cuisine tagline like "山梨県笛吹市の北イタリア料理店") or remove the title prop so Layout emits only the brand for the root path; modify the Layout invocation (the Layout component and its title prop) accordingly and, if you prefer a programmatic fix, add root-path detection inside the Layout component to emit a brand-only title when no explicit title prop is provided. In `@src/pages/robots.txt.ts`: - Around line 3-8: The GET APIRoute currently assumes the context site is defined and will throw if site is undefined; update the GET handler (export const GET) to defensively handle a missing or empty site by checking the site parameter (and/or treating it as falsy) before constructing new URL('sitemap-index.xml', site). If site is missing, return a safe default response (e.g., build robotsTxt with a fallback absolute URL or omit Sitemap) or return a 500/400 with a clear message; ensure you reference the site value only after the guard so new URL is not called with undefined. ``` </details> <details> <summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary> Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "4b0d0e0a-96d7-4f10-b296-3a18ea78f0b9"} --> Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "ff5b1114-7d8c-49e6-8ac1-43f82af23a33"} --> Create a new PR with the fixes </details> --- <details> <summary>ℹ️ Review info</summary> <details> <summary>⚙️ Run configuration</summary> **Configuration used**: defaults **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro **Run ID**: `ce6a7353-700a-4994-ae28-34603fa986ed` </details> <details> <summary>📥 Commits</summary> Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between a544daeffedc50a98b88cda60c86398dda8cf1ae and ef6c75ab6f84a863f7a38b055e9184283c082a8e. </details> <details> <summary>⛔ Files ignored due to path filters (1)</summary> * `pnpm-lock.yaml` is excluded by `!**/pnpm-lock.yaml` </details> <details> <summary>📒 Files selected for processing (7)</summary> * `astro.config.mjs` * `docs/references/astro-cloudflare-pages-seo/guidelines.md` * `docs/research/2026-04-19-astro-cloudflare-seo-best-practices.md` * `package.json` * `src/layouts/Layout.astro` * `src/pages/index.astro` * `src/pages/robots.txt.ts` </details> </details> <!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit for review status -->
**Devin Review** found 4 potential issues.
